signals.title: Trump executive order directs NASA to plan quantum space applications
signals.summary:
The White House has issued an executive order aiming to unify and accelerate U.S. development of quantum technologies, including space systems that could enable next-generation navigation, sensing and secure communications. 
 The post  Trump executive order directs NASA to plan quantum space applications  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

NASA and Boeing still uncertain about when Starliner will return to flight
signals.summary:
NASA’s safety advisers say that while the agency and Boeing make progress in addressing problems with the CST-100 Starliner, it could be up to a year before it flies again. 
 The post  NASA and Boeing still uncertain about when Starliner will return to flight  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Sens. Warren, Kelly press Trump administration on effects of tariffs on manufacturing
signals.summary: A pair of Democratic senators is asking for answers from the Trump administration on the effect of tariffs on U.S. manufacturing.

signals.title: How much electricity are 202(c) power plants producing? Way less than before.
signals.summary: The Department of Energy ordered six power plants to delay their retirements last year. Two of them produced zero electricity in the first quarter in 2026, and another one is now offline for repairs.

signals.title: Trump admits selling IBM’s stock was a mistake. Now he’s cheering its quantum future.
signals.summary: IBM looks like a focal point as the U.S. further backs domestic quantum-computing initiatives.

signals.title: Frontier Climate adds Anthropic to buyers group, makes new financing pledge
signals.summary: The company behind AI tool Claude joins the carbon removal purchasing group, which also announced $915 million in new financing commitments.

signals.title: The AI race will be won or lost on power infrastructure
signals.summary: If the harder AI constraint turns out to be infrastructure performance rather than generation, the power sector may be looking for the bottleneck in the wrong place, writes TerraFlow Energy CMO Amanda Simonian.

signals.title: Trump administration to loan $17 billion to speed deployment of 10 big nuclear reactors in U.S.
signals.summary: The loans will support the construction of five projects that each host two big nuclear reactors.

signals.title: Oracle sheds 21,000 roles over the past year amid wave of AI layoffs from tech giants
signals.summary: "The adoption and deployment of AI technologies across our operations have resulted, and may continue to result, in reductions to our workforce," Oracle said.

signals.title: ‘Trump accounts’ for newborns are nothing but a diversion
signals.summary: Take a look at this state to see what a serious effort looks like.

signals.title: GETs, demand response can ease near-term data center electricity price pressure: report
signals.summary: Data centers could use up to 15% of all U.S. electricity by 2030, up from 5% in 2024, Berkeley Lab researchers said in a separate analysis.

signals.title: 'I like their money': Trump threatens lawsuits against ABC for reporting on Reflecting Pool
signals.summary: The latest action against ABC comes as the broadcaster faces two investigations from the Federal Communications Commission.

signals.title: Trump Account signups now total more than 6 million, but millions more children are eligible
signals.summary: Trump Accounts will soon be available to millions of U.S. children; however, many may miss out on the savings opportunity — and free money.

signals.title: Sophia selects Apex bus for on-orbit computing demonstration
signals.summary:
SAN FRANCISCO – Sophia Space announced the selection June 22 of an Apex bus for its first orbital computing node. Pasadena, California-based Sophia plans to demonstrate its Thermal Integrated LEO […] 
 The post  Sophia selects Apex bus for on-orbit computing demonstration  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Frustrating Israel, fiber-optic killer drone technology has arrived in southern Lebanon
signals.summary: Analysts believe that any military engaged in conflict will, in one form or another, encounter technologies first seen in Ukraine.

signals.title: Factory job cuts in June neared financial crisis and Covid levels, S&P says
signals.summary: Though the firm's manufacturing index ran better than expected for June, it came largely from an inventory rebuild and despite sharp job cuts.

signals.title: Inside Kevin Warsh's selection process for the next Atlanta Fed president
signals.summary: Choosing a new head for the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta is an opportunity for new Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh to reshape the central bank.

signals.title: Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan to begin for ships stranded in Persian Gulf, maritime organization says
signals.summary: More than 11,000 seafarers stuck in the Persian Gulf will begin to exit through the Hormuz Strait in a large-scale evacuation plan backed by Iran and the U.S.

signals.title: SpaceX stock climbs 6%, rebounding from three-day losing streak
signals.summary: Gains have been pared back at the space and AI company following an initial surge off of its record-breaking IPO.

signals.title: Here's why we decided to put money to work in Tuesday's market pullback
signals.summary: The action on Wall Street looks far more constructive than it appeared at the crack of dawn.

signals.title: Astrobotic says sale to Voyager will allow it to scale up
signals.summary:
Lunar lander developer Astrobotic decided to sell to Voyager Technologies so it could quickly scale up to meet the projected demands of NASA’s lunar base initiative. 
 The post  Astrobotic says sale to Voyager will allow it to scale up  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Meta announces new smart glasses starting at $299, as Zuckerberg keeps pushing wearables
signals.summary: Meta executives have said they see the lightweight smart glasses as a step towards a more advanced device that includes screens in the lenses.

signals.title: U.S. issues sweeping Iran oil sanctions waivers, unlocking billions in revenue for Tehran
signals.summary: The U.S. issued a sweeping rollback of sanctions on Iranian oil, offering Tehran an economic lifeline as negotiations toward a peace deal proceed.

signals.title: AI PACs pour $20 million into New York Democratic primary in AI regulation battle
signals.summary: Companies are spending big to shape AI policies at the federal level. And the Manhattan House Democratic primary will be a test of where voters stand.

signals.title: Sen. Cassidy plans to push 'big idea' for Social Security reform in last days in office
signals.summary: As Social Security faces an imminent funding shortfall, Sen. Bill Cassidy said he has come up with a plan to invest in the stock market on the program's behalf.

signals.title: Google’s online dominance is showing signs of cracking in AI era
signals.summary: While Google remains in a position of strength in the eyes of Wall Street, the AI era is complicating the company's story.

signals.title: AI memory startup focused on cutting token costs raises $98 million
signals.summary: Engram enters the market as the AI industry grapples with a rising cost problem fueled by more expensive models.

signals.title: [13F] Berkshire Hathaway (Buffett) — nuovo filing 2009-11-05
signals.summary: Controlla il filing per vedere le nuove posizioni aperte/aumentate.

signals.title: China appears to be developing 7-meter-diameter reusable rockets
signals.summary:
A state-funded tender for tank tooling, a delivered stainless steel forging and launch pad planning suggest that China is developing 7-meter-diameter reusable rockets. 
 The post  China appears to be developing 7-meter-diameter reusable rockets  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Report: U.S. needs framework for responding to hostile acts in space
signals.summary:
Mitchell Institute workshop participants found little consensus on where competition ends and conflict begins as China expands counterspace capabilities 
 The post  Report: U.S. needs framework for responding to hostile acts in space  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: India’s three new naval ships boost maritime firepower
signals.summary: The country has made strides in constructing complex naval platforms but remains dependent on foreign suppliers for key technologies.

signals.title: Leonardo, Baykar tout in-flight control of Kizilelma drone from a M-346 jet trainer
signals.summary: Following an autonomous taxi and take-off, the Kizilelma autonomously linked to the M-346 using “an advanced radio frequency data exchange system."

signals.title: Brexit 10 years later: How the UK economy and politics changed, in charts
signals.summary: Ten years after the Brexit vote, CNBC compiled these charts show how the U.K. has changed across growth, immigration, sterling, trade and politics.

signals.title: Gold and silver tumble as rate-hike fears hit precious metals
signals.summary: Gold and silver fell Tuesday as rate-hike fears pressured precious metals and Wall Street banks reassessed their gold forecasts.

signals.title: Apollo curbs withdrawals after exit requests hit 17%, reigniting fears over private credit liquidity
signals.summary: Apollo is capping investor redemptions in its main retail-focused private credit fund after exit requests hit 17%.

signals.title: Highly successful people do 5 things to get luckier in life, says Stanford leadership expert: 'Good luck isn't random or rare'
signals.summary: Stanford University educator Tina Seelig shares what the luckiest people do every day to find success.

signals.title: Quantum computing is often seen as a risk to bitcoin. Now Trump wants to develop it.

signals.summary:
If the Trump administration is serious about executing its “America First in Africa” policy and transforming American engagement on the continent, it needs to focus on sectors where it still […]

signals.title: Major critical infrastructure disruptions are inevitable, acting CISA chief says
signals.summary: In recent years, the U.S. government has reoriented its cybersecurity strategy away from prevention and toward resilience.

signals.title: Budderfly and Viridi team up to install ‘fail-safe’ batteries at commercial facilities
signals.summary: Coupled with other efficiency measures offered through Budderfly’s energy-as-a-service solution, on-site batteries can reduce facilities’ energy expenses by up to 70%, Budderfly CEO Al Subbloie said.

signals.title: Texas, facing 438 GW queue, approves initial large-load interconnection process
signals.summary: The first projects to navigate the process will be called “Batch Zero." The Electric Reliability Council of Texas says its large-load queue is almost 90% data centers.

signals.title: 6 takeaways from FERC’s data center interconnection decision
signals.summary: If RTOs fail to address large-load concerns identified by FERC, the agency will dictate the solutions, per FERC’s David LaCerte. “I say this not as a threat, but as a statement of duty,” he said.

signals.title: EVs are already on your grid. Visibility is the missing piece.
signals.summary: The flexible loads utilities need exist on your system, but you can't manage what you can't see.

signals.title: How AI fits in the energy development workflow
signals.summary: Energy's AI adoption is low. The developers pulling ahead know which workflows to automate.

signals.title: Chinese spaceplane releases object into orbit, according to commercial space surveillance
signals.summary:
China’s secretive spaceplane has released an object into orbit during its ongoing fourth mission, according to space surveillance firm LeoLabs. 
 The post  Chinese spaceplane releases object into orbit, according to commercial space surveillance  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: US Marines in Okinawa receive first MADIS, NMESIS platforms
signals.summary: The delivery marks the service’s latest integration of modernized defenses in the increasingly contested Indo-Pacific.

signals.title: How Europe is grappling with extreme heat as red alerts issued in Britain, France, Spain and Italy
signals.summary: Several European countries issued red weather alerts as a fresh bout of extreme heat pushed temperatures beyond 40 degrees Celsius (104 degrees Fahrenheit).

signals.title: U.S. fights with Brazil for China's giant soybean market
signals.summary: The U.S. Soybean Export Council makes claims to higher quality a selling point over Brazil's crop, as American farmers attempt to win back business from the world's largest importer

signals.title: How a $4 billion Indian startup won Meta's backing but lost its founder to WhatsApp
signals.summary: Indian fintech startup Cred will raise $900 million in a round led by Meta, but has lost its founder and CEO to WhatsApp.

signals.title: Tesla faces federal probe after Model 3 slams into Texas home, killing 76-year-old
signals.summary: Harris County authorities said that the driver, Michael Butler, said that he had been using Tesla's partially automated driving systems.

signals.title: Ro Khanna challenges Elon Musk to televised debate after online DOGE battle
signals.summary: Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., has been a critic of the Elon-Musk-led Department of Government Efficiency cuts.

signals.title: China's 618 shopping festival growth slows sharply as consumer spending malaise persists
signals.summary: The latest consumer data reinforce how consumption in China remains subdued.

signals.title: 'Reward for failure': Investor support for Target Chair Brian Cornell falls to lowest level ever
signals.summary: Target Chair Brian Cornell built the retailer into a $100 billion-plus giant, but recent struggles and underperformance have fueled investor calls for change.

signals.title: Astroscale raises funding to support growth strategy
signals.summary:
Satellite servicing company Astroscale is working to transition from technology demonstrations to a regular series of missions, bolstered by a recent capital infusion. 
 The post  Astroscale raises funding to support growth strategy  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: US Army tests autonomous boats during Philippine exercise
signals.summary: Autonomous boats helped escort a U.S. Army vessel during a military exercise, in a demonstration of how uncrewed assets are changing maritime operations.

signals.title: Kyiv’s drone leverage moved the US. Moscow could be next, a top Ukrainian official says.
signals.summary: After years of needing Western weapons, Kyiv is approaching negotiations with battlefield tech the U.S. now wants — and a possible ceasefire looming.

signals.title: US couldn’t repair battle-damaged ships in war with China, study finds
signals.summary: Battle-damaged U.S. warships could not be quickly repaired and returned to combat during a war with China, according to a new study by RAND.

signals.title: Can China’s latest air-to-air missile take on its US equivalent? Definitely maybe, experts say.
signals.summary: The PL-16’s specs would let it chase large, slower-moving aircraft, one analyst said, though its effectiveness against smaller, faster planes is unclear.

signals.title: Ukraine launches ‘TrophyLab’ platform to share captured Russian weapons with allies
signals.summary: Among the listed items available for study are, for example, a Kinzhal hypersonic missile and a T-90M tank.

signals.title: Lucid to lay off roughly 18% of U.S. workforce, COO Marc Winterhoff leaves
signals.summary: Lucid Group is cutting its U.S. workforce by 18% as part of a cost-savings plan. It also said COO Marc Winterhoff is departing effective immediately.
